binaural-beats.title: Binaural Beats
binaural-beats.description: Two slightly different frequencies in each ear create a perceived beat in the brain. Requires headphones.

samples.name: Delta — 2 Hz
samples.description: Deep sleep & recovery. 2 Hz beat on 100 Hz carrier with brown noise bed.
samples.purpose: Sleep, physical recovery, deep unconscious processing
samples.research: Delta entrainment facilitates slow-wave sleep onset (Jirakittayakorn & Wongsawat, 2017)

samples.name: Theta — 6 Hz
samples.description: Meditation & creativity. 6 Hz beat on 150 Hz carrier with brown noise.
samples.research: Theta entrainment reduces anxiety and induces meditative states (Lavallee et al., 2011)

samples.name: Alpha — 10 Hz
samples.description: Calm relaxation. 10 Hz beat on 200 Hz carrier with pink noise.
samples.purpose: Stress relief, relaxed wakefulness, light meditation
samples.research: Alpha entrainment reduces cortisol levels (Huang & Charyton, 2008)

samples.name: Beta — 18 Hz
samples.description: Active focus. 18 Hz beat on 250 Hz carrier with pink noise.
samples.research: Beta entrainment supports sustained attention (Lane et al., 1998)

samples.name: Gamma — 40 Hz
samples.description: Peak cognition. 40 Hz beat on 300 Hz carrier with pink noise.
samples.research: 40 Hz gamma enhances attentional binding and memory encoding (Jirakittayakorn & Wongsawat, 2017)

isochronic-tones.title: Isochronic Tones
isochronic-tones.description: Rhythmic amplitude pulses that entrain brainwaves through sharp on/off patterns. Works through speakers — no headphones needed.

samples.name: Theta Pulse — 6 Hz
samples.description: Meditative pulsing at 6 Hz on a 180 Hz carrier with brown noise.
samples.research: Isochronic tones produce stronger cortical steady-state responses than binaural beats (Schwarz & Taylor, 2005)

samples.name: Alpha Pulse — 10 Hz
samples.description: Relaxation pulse at 10 Hz on a 250 Hz carrier with pink noise.
samples.purpose: Calm focus, stress reduction through speakers
samples.research: 10 Hz auditory stimulation increases alpha power (Calomeni et al., 2017)

samples.name: Beta Pulse — 18 Hz
samples.description: Focus pulse at 18 Hz on a 350 Hz carrier with pink noise.
samples.research: Beta-frequency auditory stimulation enhances cognitive performance (Kraus & Porubanová, 2015)

samples.name: Gamma Pulse — 40 Hz
samples.description: Cognition pulse at 40 Hz on a 400 Hz carrier with pink noise.
samples.purpose: Memory and learning enhancement through speakers
samples.research: 40 Hz auditory stimulation shows neuroprotective effects (Martorell et al., 2019 — Nature)

colored-noise.title: Colored Noise
colored-noise.description: Spectrally shaped random signals used for masking distractions and creating ambient texture.

samples.name: White Noise
samples.description: Equal energy at all frequencies. Bright, hissy texture like TV static.
samples.purpose: Maximum masking of environmental sounds, tinnitus relief
samples.research: White noise improves cognitive performance in noisy environments (Söderlund et al., 2007)

samples.name: Pink Noise
samples.description: Equal energy per octave (1/f spectrum). Balanced, natural — like steady rainfall.
samples.research: Pink noise during sleep enhances deep sleep and memory (Zhou et al., 2012)

samples.name: Brown Noise
samples.description: Low-frequency emphasis (1/f² spectrum). Deep, warm rumble like a waterfall.
samples.purpose: Deep relaxation, focus for ADHD, wind-down
samples.research: Low-frequency noise activates parasympathetic nervous system (Alvarsson et al., 2010)

ambient-drones.title: Ambient Drones
ambient-drones.description: Frequency-modulated harmonic tones that create evolving ambient textures. Subliminal harmonic anchoring for immersion.

samples.name: Deep Bass Drone
samples.description: 55 Hz sub-bass with slow FM modulation. Grounding, meditative vibration.
samples.research: Low-frequency tones activate vestibular system and promote relaxation (Todd & Cody, 2000)

samples.name: Warm Mid-Range Drone
samples.description: 130 Hz drone with gentle harmonics. Comforting, womb-like.
samples.purpose: Background comfort, stress relief, ambient fill
samples.research: Sustained tonal environments reduce physiological stress markers (Thoma et al., 2013)

samples.name: Evolving Harmonic Drone
samples.description: 200 Hz base with 5 harmonics and complex modulation. Slowly shifting timbre.
samples.research: Complex tonal environments stimulate default mode network activity (Taruffi et al., 2017)

soundscapes.title: Cognitive Soundscapes
soundscapes.description: Multi-layered compositions combining binaural beats, noise, and drones for specific cognitive states.

samples.name: Morning Energizer
samples.description: 18 Hz beta binaural + pink noise + bright drone. Wake up and activate.
samples.purpose: Morning alertness, energy boost, productive start
samples.research: Beta entrainment combined with moderate noise optimizes morning cortisol utilization

samples.name: Deep Focus
samples.description: 40 Hz gamma binaural + pink noise + subtle drone. Maximum concentration.
samples.research: Gamma entrainment enhances working memory and sustained attention

samples.name: Creative Flow
samples.description: 7 Hz theta binaural + pink noise + evolving drone. Unlock divergent thinking.
samples.research: Theta-alpha border states correlate with creative insight (Lustenberger et al., 2015)

samples.name: Study Session
samples.description: 40 Hz gamma binaural + 14 Hz beta isochronic + pink noise. Dual entrainment for learning.
samples.research: Gamma-beta coupling enhances memory encoding and retrieval

samples.name: Stress Relief
samples.description: 10 Hz alpha binaural + brown noise + warm drone. Decompress and let go.
samples.purpose: Anxiety reduction, post-work decompression, emotional regulation
samples.research: Alpha entrainment reduces state anxiety scores (Huang & Charyton, 2008)

samples.name: Power Nap
samples.description: 5 Hz theta binaural + brown noise + deep drone. 20-minute nap optimization.
samples.research: Theta entrainment accelerates sleep onset latency (Abeln et al., 2014)

samples.name: Sleep Preparation
samples.description: 2 Hz delta binaural + heavy brown noise + ultra-slow sub-bass. Drift into deep sleep.
samples.purpose: Insomnia relief, sleep onset, deep rest
samples.research: Delta entrainment with low-frequency noise promotes slow-wave sleep

samples.name: Flow State
samples.description: 10 Hz alpha binaural + 40 Hz gamma isochronic + brown noise + drone. Alpha-gamma coupling.
samples.purpose: Peak performance, athletic visualization, flow state induction
samples.research: Alpha-gamma cross-frequency coupling observed during flow (Katahira et al., 2018)

educational.title: Educational Demos
educational.description: Demonstrations that illustrate the building blocks of psychoacoustic audio engineering.

samples.name: Pure Binaural Beat (No Noise)
samples.description: Raw 10 Hz binaural beat on 200 Hz carrier. Hear the beat frequency clearly in isolation.
samples.research: The brain perceives a phantom beat at |f_right - f_left| Hz

samples.name: Pure Isochronic Tone (No Noise)
samples.description: Raw 10 Hz isochronic pulse on 300 Hz carrier. Hear the rhythmic amplitude modulation.
samples.research: Sharp amplitude onsets drive stronger neural entrainment than smooth beats

samples.name: Carrier Frequency Comparison
samples.description: Same 10 Hz beat at three carriers: 100 Hz (deep), 250 Hz (mid), 500 Hz (bright). 40s each.
samples.purpose: Hear how carrier frequency affects perceived warmth/brightness
samples.research: Lower carriers feel warmer; higher carriers feel more alert

samples.name: Noise Color Comparison
samples.description: White, pink, then brown noise — 40 seconds each. Hear the spectral differences.
samples.research: White=flat, Pink=1/f (balanced), Brown=1/f² (deep)
